seoTitle: File Schedule C in TurboTax as a Small Business
metaDescription: Learn how to file Schedule C in TurboTax to report self-employment income and expenses accurately while maximizing eligible deductions.
intro: Filing a Schedule C is a requirement for most sole proprietors and freelancers, and getting it right directly affects how much you owe or get refunded. This guide covers how to file Schedule C in TurboTax, from entering income to maximizing deductions. It's aimed at freelancers, gig workers, and small business owners navigating self-employment taxes for the first time or looking for a refresher.

steps.heading: Gather your business income and expense records
steps.body: Collect documentation of all business income and expenses for the tax year before starting your Schedule C.

steps.heading: Navigate to the Schedule C section
steps.body: Within TurboTax, locate the self-employment or business income section to begin entering your Schedule C details.

steps.heading: Enter your business income
steps.body: Input your total business income accurately, making sure it matches your records and any 1099 forms received.

steps.heading: Enter and categorize expenses
steps.body: Record your business expenses under the correct categories to ensure they're properly deducted from your income.

steps.heading: Review for maximized deductions
steps.body: Double-check commonly missed deductions, such as home office or mileage expenses, before finalizing your Schedule C.

faq.question: Who needs to file a Schedule C?
faq.answer: Sole proprietors, freelancers, and independent contractors who earn self-employment income generally need to file a Schedule C.

faq.question: What deductions are commonly missed on Schedule C?
faq.answer: Home office expenses, mileage, and business-related subscriptions are frequently overlooked deductions worth reviewing carefully.

faq.question: Does TurboTax automate any part of the Schedule C process?
faq.answer: Yes, TurboTax can help calculate totals and suggest deductions based on the information you enter.

faq.question: Can side income from a hobby be reported on Schedule C?
faq.answer: Generally, Schedule C is for business activity conducted with the intent to earn a profit, not casual hobby income.
